mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower the discount rate for future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates a structural shift in consumer demand away from interest-rate-sensitive sectors, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening aggregate supply and corporate output capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or the predictive power vanishes in high-inflation sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.01, or the signal loses predictive power during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are non-negative in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth across production and consumption channels, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition fails to yield a positive directional accuracy > 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high-rate environment signals demand destruction from credit constraints, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high-rate periods predicts positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in this regime.
